q: A plant disease is usually defined as normal growth. 2025
Biotic diseases are caused by non-living environmental conditions. 2025
Nematodes are very large roundworms. 2025
Study of plant diseases is called plant pathology. 2025

Abiotic diseases are caused by non-living environmental conditions. 2025
Most plant diseases, around 85 percent are caused by bacteria and viruses. 2025
Disease may also reduce yield and quality of harvested product. 2024
Plant disease is any abnormal condition that alters the appearance or function of a plant. 2024, 2026
Abiotic diseases are caused by living organisms. 2026
Chemical injury will likely cause damage on different types of plants. 2025
The fungi that cause tomato leaf blight do not cause damage on sweet corn. 2025
Pseudomonas bacteria can cause leaf spot of tobacco, blight of peas and soybeans. 2026
Signs of plant disease are physiological evidence of the pathogen. 2024

All plants are attacked by one species or another of phytopathogenic bacteria and viruses. 2024
All bacteria are bad for plants and soil. 2024

Plant parasitic nematodes have a stylet, a piercing mouthpart. 2024
Chemicals used to control fungi are called fungicides. 2025
Most cardiovascular disease starts with atheroma formation. 2026
Measuring blood pressure is used as an indicator of both the heart and blood vessels. 2026
Warfarin and heparin are a class of medicines used to reduce blood clotting. 2026
A stroke is caused by an interruption to the normal blood supply to an area of the heart. 2025
A healthy blood pressure is around 120 mmHg during diastole and around 80 mmHg during systole. 2025
Platelet inhibitory drugs such as aspirin are also a type of anticoagulant. 2025
Non-infectious diseases are those that are not caused by pathogens. 2025
The hardening of arteries, caused by atheroma, is called angina. 2025
Oxygen is supplied to the liver muscle by the coronary arteries. 2025
DVT is the formation of blood clot in a vein deep outside the body. 2025
Oxygen is supplied to the heart muscle by the coronary arteries. 2025
The development of atherosclerosis is linked to many types of cancers. 2024
Sedentary lifestyle can lead to heart diseases. 2024
The narrowed coronary arteries supply enough oxygenated blood and the heart muscle resorts to anaerobic respiration. 2024
Most cases of malaria are caused by one of five species of the protist Plasmodium. 2025
The parasites enter the white blood cells, where they multiply. 2025
Plasmodium can also pass across the placenta from mother to foetus. 2025
Plasmodium multiplies only in single hosts. 2025
The best protection against malaria is to avoid being bitten. 2025
